chapter_context.summary: Pronounces a woe on the self-satisfied, proud leaders at ease in Zion and Samaria who lounge on ivory beds, drink wine in bowls, and compose useless music while ignoring the ruin of Joseph, prophesying that they shall go captive with the first who go.

outline.text: Woe to the Complacent Elite Loungeing on Ivory Beds and Anointing Themselves with Chief Ointments
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Woe to the Complacent Elite Loungeing on Ivory Beds and Anointing Themselves with Chief Ointments (6:1-7), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Amos.

outline.text: God's Abhorrence of Jacob's Excellency; Absolute Destruction of Houses and Cities
outline.explanation: This section focuses on God's Abhorrence of Jacob's Excellency; Absolute Destruction of Houses and Cities (6:8-11), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Amos.

outline.text: Rebuking Perversion of Justice and Boasting in Physical Might; The Threat of an Invading Nation
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Rebuking Perversion of Justice and Boasting in Physical Might; The Threat of an Invading Nation (6:12-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Amos.

key_themes.title: Social Justice and Equity
key_themes.description: Amos fiercely denounces religious ritual that exists alongside economic exploitation, emphasizing that true worship requires justice for the poor.

key_themes.title: The Danger of Spiritual Complacency
key_themes.description: The book warns that material prosperity can easily blind a nation to its spiritual and ethical decline, leading to swift judgment.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the indictments and visions of Chapter 6, how can you actively champion justice and care for the needy in your community today?
How can we guard ourselves against the kind of complacency and empty religious formalism that Amos rebuked in Samaria?

cultural_historical.title: The Reign of Jeroboam II
cultural_historical.text: Amos prophesied during the mid-8th century BC, a period of remarkable material prosperity, territory expansion, and luxurious stability for the Northern Kingdom under Jeroboam II, which unfortunately saw extreme wealth disparity and systemic social oppression.
